content.text: All 16 tests pass. The fix wraps bare HTML fragments in `<html><body>...</body></html>` before parsing, preventing the `null.tagName` crash in Readability's DOM traversal.

message.content: alright let's go ahead and commit these changes abd study the repo, what is the next set of actions automations to take place.

content.text: Let me commit first, then study the repo.

input.command:
git add src/extract.ts mod_test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: normalise HTML fragments before parsing to prevent Readability crash

Readability walks up the DOM via parentNode until it finds a BODY element.
When input is a bare HTML fragment (e.g. Confluence REST API body.export_view),
linkedom returns null at the document root, causing null.tagName to throw.

Wrap any input that doesn't start with <!DOCTYPE or <html> in a minimal
<html><body> shell inside parseDocument() — the single entry point for all
HTML into the pipeline.
EOF
)"
